text: Try starting it in the foreground to see the real error: `sudo /usr/sbin/sshd -D -e` (Ctrl-C after a moment). It won't just say 'failed' -- read the actual output.

It complains about each host key individually before giving up entirely: `Permissions 0644 for '/etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key' are too open... This private key will be ignored.` Check all three: `ls -l /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key`.

sshd refuses to use any private key file that's readable by group/other -- and once every host key is rejected, it has literally nothing left to identify itself with, so it exits outright rather than starting without one.
Fix the permissions on all of them: `sudo chmod 600 /etc/ssh/ssh_host_*_key`, then start the service: `sudo service ssh start`.
